channel.title: The Olsen Tribune
channel.description: A one-person newsletter on the business of pharmacy benefit managers. Primary sources, plain English, one careful read each week.

item.title: The Pharmacy Desert Is a PBM Business Model
item.description: Nearly one in three U.S. retail pharmacies have closed since 2010, and the contraction is accelerating in 2026. Two FTC staff reports trace the squeeze to a small set of contract terms that move money from independents to pharmacies the PBMs own.
item.author: colsen8128@gmail.com (Chris Olsen)

item.title: The Copay Loophole States Tried to Close
item.description: Six states forced insurers to start counting drug-copay assistance toward patient deductibles in 2025. The same insurers are running the same playbook in four new states in 2026. The names on the violator list don't change. The states do.
item.author: colsen8128@gmail.com (Chris Olsen)

item.title: What the FTC actually locked in
item.description: Reading the Express Scripts decree, the agency didn't blow up the pharmacy-benefit-manager business model. It made the existing model the opt-out option. Here's what changes, what doesn't, and where the savings actually come from.
item.author: colsen8128@gmail.com (Chris Olsen)
